rulings.Case Title: HAROLD GERECKE et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION, LTD et al
rulings.Rulings: Line 1. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corp.¿s motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1 and 2 (general negligence excluding negligent failure to warn, strict liability design and manufacturing defect), off calendar as to issues 3 and 4 (false representation and civil battery) and denied as to issue 5 (punitive damages). As to issues 1 and 2, defendant satisfied its burden of production of making a prima facie case that it is entitled to prevail on its government contractor defense as to non-duty to warn claims and plaintiffs have not presented admissible evidence creating a triable issue on this defense. As to punitive damages, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs have dismissed their false representation and civil battery causes of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to B & P Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost. Any party who contests a tentative must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative is not contested, the prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance w/ CRC3.1312

rulings.Case Title: HAROLD GERECKE et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION, LTD et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 21, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Spirax Sarco Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed August 7. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GENE LEPORE VS. AC AND S INC et al
rulings.Rulings: Line 2. Defendant Nacco Materials Handling Group¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages is off calendar. The deposition testimony of Mr. Bangs and Mr. Libby create a triable issue whether Mr. Lepore was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s separate statement failed to properly make an issue of substantial factor causation, but even if it had, Dr. Horn¿s declaration would create a triable issue whether Mr. Lepore¿s exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his asbestos-related illness. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies. The Navy¿s knowledge of the hazards of asbestos cannot be imputed to Mr. Lepore. Plaintiffs dismissed their claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to B&P Code Sec 8016. If the parties are unable to agree, the court will designate a qualified reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost. Any party who contests a tentative must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm. If a tentative is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative is not contested, the prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance CRC 3.1312. =
